ddls:
CREATE TABLE "addresses" (
  "address_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each address */,
  "address_area" TEXT /* The name or names of a geographic area or locality that groups a number of addressable objects for addressing purposes, without being an administrative unit */,
  "administrative_unit_level_1" TEXT /* The name or names of a unit of administration where a Member State has and/or exercises jurisdictional rights, for local, regional and national governance. Level 1 refers to the uppermost administrative unit for the address, almost always a country */,
  "administrative_unit_level_2" TEXT /* The name or names of a unit of administration where a Member State has and/or exercises jurisdictional rights, for local, regional and national governance. Level 2 refers to the region of the address, usually a county, state, or other such area that typically encompasses several localities */,
  "full_address" TEXT /* The complete address written as a formatted string */,
  "street_number" TEXT /* The street number of the address */,
  "postal_code" TEXT /* The postal code of the address */,
  "latitude" TEXT /* The latitude of the address */,
  "longitude" TEXT /* The longitude of the address */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the address was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the address was last updated */,
  "guid" TEXT /* Globally unique identifier for each instance of an address */,
  "address_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the address type */,
  "default_language_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the default language of the address */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("address_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_addresses_administrative_unit_level_1 FOREIGN KEY ("administrative_unit_level_1") REFERENCES administrative_unit_levels_1 ("unit_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_addresses_address_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("address_type_id") REFERENCES address_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_addresses_default_language_id FOREIGN KEY ("default_language_id") REFERENCES address_languages ("language_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_addresses_address_area FOREIGN KEY ("address_area") REFERENCES address_areas ("area_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_addresses_administrative_unit_level_2 FOREIGN KEY ("administrative_unit_level_2") REFERENCES administrative_unit_levels_2 ("unit_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "address_areas" (
  "area_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each geographic area */,
  "area_name" TEXT /* Name of the geographic area or locality */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the geographic area or locality */,
  "area_type" TEXT /* Type of geographic area (e.g., city, town, village) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("area_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "administrative_unit_levels_1" (
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each administrative unit at level 1 */,
  "unit_name" TEXT /* Name of the administrative unit at level 1 (country) */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO 3166-1 code for the country */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the administrative unit at level 1 */,
  "currency_code" TEXT /* Currency code for the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "administrative_unit_levels_2" (
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each administrative unit at level 2 */,
  "unit_name" TEXT /* Name of the administrative unit at level 2 (region/state) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the administrative unit at level 2 */,
  "parent_unit_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the parent administrative unit at level 1 (country) */,
  "region_type" TEXT /* Type of region (e.g., state, province, county) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "address_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each history entry */,
  "address_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the address that was changed */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change (e.g., update, delete) */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the change */,
  "change_description" TEXT /* Description of the change */,
  "changed_by_user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who made the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_address_history_changed_by_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("changed_by_user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_address_history_address_id FOREIGN KEY ("address_id") REFERENCES addresses ("address_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "address_languages" (
  "language_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each language entry */,
  "address_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the address */,
  "language_code" TEXT /* ISO 639-1 code for the language */,
  "language_name" TEXT /* Name of the language */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("language_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_address_languages_address_id FOREIGN KEY ("address_id") REFERENCES addresses ("address_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "address_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each address type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the address type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the address type */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Is this the default address type? */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "address_usage" (
  "usage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each usage entry */,
  "address_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the address */,
  "usage_context" TEXT /* Context in which the address is used */,
  "usage_description" TEXT /* Description of the usage context */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the usage */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the usage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("usage_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_address_usage_address_id FOREIGN KEY ("address_id") REFERENCES addresses ("address_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, manager, user) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "address_id" INTEGER /* ID of the address being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the address */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the address was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit, delete) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the address */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_address_id FOREIGN KEY ("address_id") REFERENCES addresses ("address_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "address_codelists" (
  "codelist_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each codelist entry */,
  "codelist_name" TEXT /* Name of the codelist */,
  "codelist_value" TEXT /* Value of the codelist entry */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the codelist entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("codelist_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "address_audits" (
  "audit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit entry */,
  "address_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the address that was changed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the action */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action (e.g., create, update, delete) */,
  "action_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the action */,
  "action_description" TEXT /* Description of the action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("audit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_address_audits_address_id FOREIGN KEY ("address_id") REFERENCES addresses ("address_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_address_audits_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "address_validation" (
  "validation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each validation entry */,
  "address_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the address that was validated */,
  "validation_date" TEXT /* Date when the address was validated */,
  "is_valid" INTEGER /* Is the address valid? */,
  "validation_message" TEXT /* Validation message */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("validation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_address_validation_address_id FOREIGN KEY ("address_id") REFERENCES addresses ("address_id")
)
column_names: Unique identifier for each address
The name or names of a geographic area or locality that groups a number of addressable objects for addressing purposes, without being an administrative unit
